title: Elections, Parties, and Representation
shortSummary: Understand how Australians choose representatives, how parties organise power, and why the Senate, crossbench, and preferences matter so much in practice.
whyItMatters: Politics is not only institutions; it is also the competition for authority inside them. This module explains how representation and party incentives shape what governments can actually do.
mentalModels: Votes create representation, but parties aggregate power
The Senate often changes what the House wants to do
Preferences and the crossbench turn margins into leverage

goDeeper.label: Open current politics signals
goDeeper.description: See how elections, parties, and institutions collide in current public life.

goDeeper.label: Continue into Cabinet and the APS
goDeeper.description: Follow representation into executive power and administrative delivery.
